title: parahebe birleyi
intro: Parahebe birleyi, also known as the New Zealand spinach or 'Punihou', is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Parahebe birleyi include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, with the leaves typically being dried and used in these forms.
